methodology.note: Constraint counts are exact R1CS counts compiled with circom. Verifier linearization gas is analytical: 6150 gas per public input (ECMUL + ECADD under EIP-196). Prover-time estimates scale the measured current proof median by total constraint ratio.

rows.name: current full-limb Poseidon bridge
rows.securityStatus: Injective full-coverage decomposition over the canonical 6144-bit ciphertext space
rows.caveat: High public-input count and calldata footprint are the price of board-visible injectivity

rows.name: SHA-256-in-circuit bridge
rows.securityStatus: Full ciphertext coverage, but security depends on SHA-256 collision resistance and digest packing
rows.caveat: Much lower public-input cost, but the circuit itself becomes very large

rows.name: Pedersen chunk-commit proxy (24 x 256-bit chunks)
rows.securityStatus: Full coverage only after chunking the ciphertext into 24 Pedersen commitments
rows.caveat: This is the closest measurable same-group proxy, not a drop-in bulletin-board replacement: the board would still need to carry and reconcile 48 public commitment coordinates

rows.name: partial-limb binding (4 limbs)
rows.securityStatus: Structurally non-injective on the canonical ciphertext space
rows.caveat: Cheaper because it leaves 5376 bits unauthenticated; the ablation shows concrete prefix ambiguity
